text: The patient is intubated with a double lumen endotracheal tilt and positioned with a left lateral decubitus position.
The uniportal incision is located at the fifth intercostal space, anterior to the latissimus dorsi muscle and posterior to the pectoralis major.
A worm protector is put into the incision.
We firstly dissect level 10 and level 11 lymph nodes, which will facilitate the subsequent dissection of vasculars.
The level 10 lymph nodes has been dissected.
Level 10 lymph nodes is bonded by the superior vena cava, esgus vein, and the right upper lobe vein.
Here we dissect the level 11 lymph nodes which will facilitate the subsequent transsection of the posterior arsenic artery.
We continue to complete dissect the major fissure and dissect all the level 11 lymph nodes to transact the posterior ascending artery.
all the level 11 lymph nodes has been dissected and now the posterior ascending is well exposed and we dissect the posterior ascending artery.
The next step, we will transact the bronchus.
So firstly, we will dissect the lymph nodes around the right upper lobe bronchus for subsequent transaction of the bronchus.
Now the right upper lobe bronchus is well exposed and we transact the right upper lobe bronchus.
Then we expose the apical segment artery and transect the apical segment artery.
Then we transact the lobar node which is located between the pulmonary artery and the bronchus.
When this node is dissected, we will expose the artery and the vein very easily and it will be easier for us to transact the remnant vascular.
And now we can actually use one stapler to transect the artery and the vein together.
And all the whole procedure we transect posteriorly to anteriorly.
We put a lap sac to take the sample out.
Then we will perform
Midastinal lymph node dissection, firstly we conduct subclinal lymph node dissection.
We firstly use the hooked electrocautery to demonstrate a clear margin, then we combine with a harmonic scalpel to minimize bleeding.
Now the subcrinal lymph node has been dissected.
We can clearly show you the esophagus, the right main stem branch, and the esgus vein, and the pericardios here.
All the lymph nodes have been dissected.
We firstly dissect the lymph node under the esgus vein.
This will facilitate the subsequent transsection of the superior
mediastinal lymph node.
And then we will dissect the superior mediastinal lymph node, which is named level 2 and level 4.
The level 2 and level 4 lymph nodes have been dissected.
The lymph nodes are bordered by esgus vein inferiorly, enamel artery superiorly, trachea posteriorly, and superior vena cava anteriorly.
The whole procedure was smooth and with no obvious woozing.
We put a 28 French chest tube from the incision and a pigtail tube from the AS
